meta.title: OxideTerm — Pure Rust SSH Terminal
meta.description: A cross-platform SSH terminal built with Rust and Tauri. Local shells, SSH, SFTP, IDE, AI, port forwarding — all in one native binary.

hero.tagline: Zero Electron. Zero OpenSSL. Pure Rust SSH.
hero.subtitle: One native binary — local shells, SSH, SFTP, remote IDE, AI, port forwarding, plugins, 30+ themes, 11 languages.

features.title: Built Different
features.rust_ssh_desc: Built on russh with ring crypto backend. Zero C dependencies, zero OpenSSL. Compiled entirely in safe, auditable Rust.
features.native_desc: 25–40 MB binary powered by Tauri 2.0. Not an Electron wrapper — genuine native app with Rust backend and WebGL-accelerated terminal.
features.allinone_desc: Terminal, SFTP, IDE, AI assistant, port forwarding, and plugins — unified in a single window. No more juggling tools.
features.reconnect_desc: Network drops? OxideTerm probes your old connection for 30 seconds before reconnecting. Your vim, htop, and yazi sessions survive.

comparison.title: Why OxideTerm?
comparison.subtitle: Real problems, real solutions.
comparison.pain_1: SSH clients that can't do local shells
comparison.answer_1: Hybrid engine — local PTY (zsh/bash/fish/pwsh/WSL2) + remote SSH in one window
comparison.answer_2: Grace Period reconnect — probes old connection for 30s before killing it. Your vim, htop, yazi survive
comparison.pain_3: Remote file editing needs VS Code Remote
comparison.answer_3: Built-in IDE — CodeMirror 6 over SFTP with 30+ languages, optional ~1 MB remote agent
comparison.answer_4: Multiplexing — one SSH connection shared across terminal, SFTP, forwarding, and IDE
comparison.answer_5: russh 0.54 — pure Rust SSH compiled against ring. Zero C dependencies
comparison.answer_6: Tauri 2.0 — 25–40 MB binary. Not a wrapper — genuine native app with Rust backend
comparison.answer_7: OxideSens — 40+ autonomous tools, MCP protocol, RAG knowledge base. Works with any API

showcase.title: See It in Action
showcase.terminal_desc: Split panes, broadcast input, WebGL rendering at 60fps+, 30+ built-in themes, session recording, and command palette.
showcase.sftp_desc: Dual-pane file browser with drag-and-drop, smart preview for images, video, code, PDF, and hex. Transfer queue with progress tracking.
showcase.ide_desc: CodeMirror 6 editor with 30+ language modes, file tree with Git status, conflict resolution, and integrated terminal.
showcase.forwarding_desc: Local (-L), Remote (-R), and Dynamic SOCKS5 (-D) forwarding. Lock-free I/O, auto-restore on reconnect, death reporting.
showcase.ai_desc: OxideSens AI with 40+ autonomous tools, MCP server integration, RAG knowledge base, multi-source context capture.

tech.title: Under the Hood
tech.dual_plane_desc: WebSocket binary frames for terminal I/O, Tauri IPC for control commands. Each session gets dedicated bandwidth.
tech.webgl_desc: GPU-accelerated terminal via xterm.js 6. Adaptive scheduler: 120Hz+ burst, 60Hz normal, 1–15Hz idle backoff.
tech.pool_desc: One SSH connection shared across terminal, SFTP, forwarding, and IDE. Reference-counted pool with automatic cleanup.
tech.encryption_desc: ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD with Argon2id KDF (256 MB memory, 4 iterations). OS keychain for passwords, Touch ID gate on macOS.
tech.i18n_desc: Full UI translation across 22 namespaces: English, 简体中文, 繁體中文, 日本語, 한국어, Français, Deutsch, Español, Italiano, Português, Tiếng Việt.
tech.plugin_desc: Runtime ESM loading with 18 API namespaces, 24 pre-built UI components, frozen API contract, and circuit breaker auto-disable.

download.title: Download OxideTerm
download.subtitle: Available for macOS, Windows, and Linux

demo.title: Watch It in Action
demo.subtitle: OxideSens AI opens a local terminal and runs a command — fully autonomous, fully integrated.
demo.fallback: Your browser does not support video playback.

details.title: Everything You Need, Nothing You Don't
details.terminal_1: Split panes with broadcast input to all sessions
details.terminal_2: WebGL rendering at 60fps+ with 120Hz burst mode
details.terminal_4: 31+ themes with a full CSS variable custom theme editor
details.terminal_6: Configurable keyboard shortcuts with vi-mode support
details.terminal_7: Auto-complete for SSH hostnames and saved sessions
details.sftp_2: Smart preview: images, video, audio, code, PDF, hex, fonts
details.sftp_3: Transfer queue with real-time progress & ETA
details.sftp_5: Rename, chmod, chown with batch operations
details.sftp_6: Watch mode with auto-upload on local changes
details.ide_2: File tree with Git status indicators
details.ide_5: Search & replace with regex across remote files
details.ide_6: Syntax highlighting for 30+ languages: Rust, Python, JS/TS, Go, C/C++, Java, YAML, JSON, TOML, Markdown, Shell, and more
details.ai_3: RAG knowledge base (BM25 + vector hybrid search)
details.ai_4: Multi-source context capture: terminal, IDE, SFTP, Git
details.ai_5: Bring your own key — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or any compatible endpoint

security.title: Security First
security.subtitle: Enterprise-grade protection without enterprise complexity.
security.encryption_desc: ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD with Argon2id KDF (256 MB memory, 4 iterations) for .oxide encrypted exports.
security.keychain_desc: Passwords and API keys stored in macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, or Linux Secret Service. Never in config files.
security.ssh_desc: russh 0.54 with ring crypto backend. Ed25519, RSA, ECDSA keys. ChaCha20-Poly1305 and AES-GCM cipher suites.
security.biometric_desc: Touch ID gates keychain access on macOS. Host key verification with Trust-On-First-Use (TOFU). Sensitive memory zeroized on drop.
security.zeroize_desc: All sensitive data — passwords, keys, decrypted payloads — is securely erased from memory via the zeroize crate. Drop handlers guarantee no lingering secrets.
security.ws_token_desc: Each WebSocket bridge receives a unique, single-use token with a time limit. Prevents replay attacks and unauthorized session hijacking.

cli.title: CLI Companion
cli.subtitle: Meet oxt — your terminal wingman.
cli.desc: JSON-RPC 2.0 over Unix Socket (macOS/Linux) or Named Pipe (Windows). Human-friendly and machine-parseable output.

competitive.title: What's Included
competitive.subtitle: Every feature ships built-in. No plugins required, no add-ons to buy.

legal.title: Compliance & Legal
legal.subtitle: OxideTerm is designed for legitimate system administration, development, and research. We take compliance seriously.
legal.network_desc: OxideTerm is a professional remote server management and terminal emulation tool. This software is intended solely for compliant system administration, network management, software development, and academic research. Users must strictly comply with all applicable laws and regulations when using SSH tunneling or port forwarding features. Any use of this software for unauthorized access to restricted resources or violation of cybersecurity regulations is strictly prohibited.
legal.ai_desc: The AI-assisted features provided by OxideTerm (such as command completion and log analysis) function as technical integration tools. AI conversation history is stored locally on the user's device using encrypted storage and is never uploaded to any server. The software does not distribute AI-generated content to third parties. Users are responsible for configuring their own third-party AI service API credentials and bear ultimate legal responsibility for the resulting interactions. We recommend using these features in compliance with all applicable AI governance regulations in your jurisdiction.
legal.data_desc: We understand the sensitivity of operational data. OxideTerm adopts a Local-First architecture. All server credentials, keys, and configuration files are encrypted and stored exclusively on the user's local device using high-strength encryption algorithms. This software does not collect, upload, or disclose any sensitive connection data to third parties.
legal.license_desc: This software is distributed under the Source Available (PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0) license. Users may view the source code and use it for non-commercial purposes. Commercial use requires explicit authorization from the developer.
legal.disclaimer: This page is provided for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Users are responsible for ensuring their use of OxideTerm complies with all applicable local and international laws.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: OxideTerm is source-available under the PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 license. Free for personal, educational, and non-commercial use. Commercial use requires a separate license.
faq.q2: Which SSH authentication methods are supported?
faq.a2: Password, private key (Ed25519, RSA, ECDSA), keyboard-interactive, agent forwarding, and FIDO2/U2F hardware security keys.
faq.a3: Everything stays on your device. Credentials are stored in your OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service). Exported configs use ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption. Nothing is uploaded to any server.
faq.a4: Yes. OxideTerm is a local-first application with zero cloud dependency. All features work offline except AI chat, which requires an internet connection to reach your configured API provider.
faq.q5: What do I need for AI features?
faq.a5: Bring your own API key (BYOK). OxideTerm supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Keys are stored securely in your OS keychain.
faq.q6: Why doesn't OxideTerm use libssh2 or OpenSSL?
faq.a6: Three reasons. First, memory safety: OpenSSL's C codebase has produced critical CVEs like Heartbleed — Rust's ownership model eliminates that entire class of bug. Second, zero deployment friction: libssh2 requires each target platform to have C dev headers and the system OpenSSL matching in version (1.0 vs 1.1 vs 3.0 have incompatible ABIs); russh ships as pure Rust source and Cargo handles everything. Third, async-native performance: libssh2 is a blocking C API that needs wrapper glue for Tokio; russh is async-first with zero FFI boundary in the hot path. The result: a single Rust binary that compiles to 10+ Linux architectures without a C toolchain — and a crypto stack you can actually audit.

testimonial.quote: OxideTerm wasn't built to fill a market gap — it was built to challenge the boundary between terminal performance and capability. Pure-Rust SSH with zero C dependencies. A custom binary wire protocol with no JSON serialization in the hot path. ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption backed by Argon2id at 256 MB memory cost. Every architectural decision reflects one obsession: never trade correctness for convenience.
testimonial.name: AnalyseDeCircuit
